title: Do you use monday for real estate asset or work order management?
excerpt:
:waving_hand: Hey monday community—if you’re using your account to manage real estate workflows (or thinking about it), we’ve been working on something you might find helpful.

posts.author: Sammy (Samantha)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey monday community—if you’re using your account to manage real estate workflows (or thinking about it), we’ve been working on something you might find helpful.

We’ve been building out solution for real estate teams to manage physical assets, CapEx planning, work orders, and preventative maintenance—all inside monday CRM and monday service.

In our upcoming short but to the point webinar, we’re walking through how we:
Track HVACs, roofs, flooring, etc. across multiple properties
Manage warranties and schedule recurring maintenance
Log repair history and budget for capital improvements
Link asset management with transaction tracking for better ROI visibility

We’ll also show how the setup connects across boards using automations, dashboards, forms, and views you might already be using—so you can adapt it to your own workflows, even if you’re not in real estate.

Tuesday, June 17 @ 12:00 EST
Open Q&A at the end— send questions ahead of time to sam@purpleplumtech.com (we promise to reach out to you with answers even if we don’t get to the question during the webinar).

Register here

Would love to hear from others managing physical assets or distributed operations—what’s working well for you inside monday?

posts.author: carlbidwell (Carl Bidwell)
posts.cookedHtml: That’s a smart setup! Bringing asset tracking, maintenance, and CapEx planning into monday keeps everything in one place, which is a huge time-saver. I’m especially interested in how you’ve linked it with transaction tracking—it feels like a game changer for visibility and ROI.

posts.author: Sammy (Samantha)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Carl, I’m not allowed to share the link to the webinar here, but if you visit Purple Plum Consulting’s YT page you can see the full webinar from June (search: Managing real estate marketing with PageProof) - we’d love to hear your thoughts1
